meta.title: CamCon — Film-look photos on your phone, the instant you shoot
meta.desc: CamCon links your DSLR or mirrorless camera to Android over USB or Wi-Fi and delivers film-simulated photos to your phone the moment you press the shutter. A cinema camera controller.
hero.title: Press the shutter, and the film-look photo lands on your phone
hero.sub: CamCon — Cinema Camera Controller. Connect your camera over USB or Wi-Fi and receive photos with a film simulation already applied, right as you shoot.
features.title: From capture to color, bridged between camera and phone
feat.wifi.d: Receive photos instantly over PTP/IP, no cable needed. It reconnects automatically if the link drops and keeps receiving in the background.
feat.usb.d: A rock-solid wired connection built on standard PTP. Plug in a USB-C (OTG) cable and your camera is recognized instantly.
feat.film.d: 3D LUTs modeled on real film stocks, applied automatically to every shot. You receive the photo with the film look already baked in.
feat.color.d: Pull the color from a single reference photo you love and apply it to your own shots.
feat.live.d: Watch a real-time preview while adjusting ISO, shutter, aperture and white balance. Choose from three quality levels: speed, balance, quality.
feat.timelapse.d: Set an interval for unattended long-duration shoots. Release remotely with zero camera shake.
feat.raw.d: Download RAW originals (NEF and more) losslessly. The film look is applied only to a JPG copy, so your original stays untouched.
feat.auto.d: Just power on your camera — CamCon discovers and reconnects it automatically via mDNS/SSDP. No setup every time.
films.title: 9 families, 296 film simulations
films.lead: From black & white to instant and color negative — real film tone curves and color, reproduced as 3D LUTs.
films.freeLabel: Free Signature — 5 signature films, free to use
films.baHint: Drag the handle to compare the original and the film look.
cameras.title: Will my camera work?
cameras.lead: If your camera supports USB (PTP) or Wi-Fi STA (router) mode, it's supported.
cameras.usbDesc: PTP-compatible cameras connected with a USB-C (OTG) cable. Supports major makers — Nikon, Canon, Sony, Fujifilm, Panasonic, OM System (Olympus), Pentax, Ricoh, Leica and more.
cameras.wifiDesc: Wireless (Wi-Fi) connection is currently verified on Nikon models and is expanding. Other makers can connect right away over USB, and features like capture, transfer and film looks work the same regardless of how you connect.
cameras.unified: Whether you connect over USB or Wi-Fi, every feature — capture, transfer, film looks — is identical.
cameras.listNote: The list below shows PTP (USB) compatible models. Wi-Fi depends on whether the camera supports STA mode.
cameras.searchPh: Search by brand or model (e.g. Nikon Z8)
cameras.help: Most cameras that support a PTP/MTP USB connection will work. If you're unsure, contact us and we'll check for you.
connect.title: Three ways to connect
connect.usb1: Connect your phone and camera with a USB-C (OTG) cable.
connect.usb2: Allow the USB connection permission in the app.
connect.usb3: Once it's recognized and connected, start shooting right away.
connect.usbPerm: Permission needed — USB device access (allowed once on connection)
connect.ap1: Turn on Wi-Fi (access point) mode in your camera's menu.
connect.ap2: Connect your phone's Wi-Fi to the network the camera creates.
connect.ap3: The app discovers and connects to the camera automatically.
connect.apPerm: Permissions needed — Location (to scan nearby Wi-Fi), Notifications (for background receiving)
connect.sta1: Connect the camera and phone to the same router.
connect.sta2: Search for and select the camera in the app.
connect.sta3: On the first connection, approve "Allow connection" on the camera body. After that it reconnects automatically.
connect.staPerm: Permissions needed — Location (to scan the network), Notifications (for background receiving)
pricing.title: Start free, go PRO when you need to
pricing.lead: Start free. The PRO plan is coming soon.
faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: How do I check whether my camera is supported?
faq.a1: If your camera supports USB (PTP) or Wi-Fi STA (router) mode, it's supported. There's no difference in features depending on how you connect. You can search for your model under "Browse all supported models" above.
faq.q2: Will my RAW files be altered?
faq.a2: RAW originals are preserved as-is. The film simulation and color are applied only to a JPG copy; RAW files (NEF and more) are never modified.
faq.q3: Does it keep receiving in the background?
faq.a3: It runs as a foreground service, so it keeps receiving while you use other apps. Receiving stops only if you fully close the app.
faq.q4: Which Android version do I need?
faq.a4: Android 10 or later on a 64-bit (arm64) device.
faq.q5: Where do I enter a referral code?
faq.a5: You can enter it on the login screen, or under Settings → Register referral code.
cta.title: A workflow where the shot is the result
cta.sub: The instant you press the shutter, the film-look photo arrives on your phone.
